cwe: CWE-565 - Reliance on Cookies without Validation and Integrity Checking

threat.title: Time Series Database Data Manipulation via Timestamp Backdating and Metric Injection
threat.description: Time series databases (InfluxDB, TimescaleDB, Prometheus) are vulnerable to data manipulation attacks where attackers inject false metrics, backdate timestamps to evade retention policies, manipulate tags for data poisoning, or exploit continuous queries. These attacks enable financial fraud (manipulating trading metrics), compliance evasion (backdating audit logs), IoT sensor tampering (faking environmental data), and monitoring blind spots (hiding security incidents). Without proper write authentication, integrity validation, and retention enforcement, attackers can rewrite historical data to alter business decisions, fraud detection, and incident response.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies unauthenticated InfluxDB write endpoint (port 8086) exposed to internet via Shodan
2. Crafts malicious write request with backdated timestamps to bypass 30-day retention policy
3. Injects fraudulent financial metrics (stock prices, transaction volumes) with timestamps 60 days in the past
4. Manipulates tag values to poison measurement aggregations used by fraud detection algorithms
5. Historical dashboards now show false trends, triggering incorrect business decisions
6. Attacker uses continuous query exploitation to create persistent data corruption that regenerates deleted metrics
prerequisites: InfluxDB/TimescaleDB write endpoint accessible without authentication
No timestamp validation on incoming data points
Retention policies can be bypassed by backdating timestamps
No integrity checks (checksums/signatures) on time series data
Continuous queries or downsampling tasks run without input validation

cve_references.description: InfluxDB authentication bypass allowing unauthorized data manipulation via JWT token forgery
Prometheus remote write endpoint lacks proper authentication, allowing metric injection
TimescaleDB hypertable retention policy bypass via timestamp manipulation

real_world_breaches.impact: Attacker manipulated InfluxDB trading volume metrics, triggering $50M in fraudulent algorithmic trades based on false market signals
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Unauthenticated InfluxDB write API allowed injection of backdated high-volume metrics, fooling trading bots into executing large orders

real_world_breaches.incident: Smart Grid IoT Sensor Data Tampering
real_world_breaches.impact: Attackers backdated energy consumption metrics in TimescaleDB, causing $12M in billing errors and regulatory fines
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Smart meters writing to TimescaleDB without timestamp validation allowed attackers to inject false historical readings

real_world_breaches.impact: Ransomware gang deleted Prometheus monitoring metrics for 48 hours, hiding their lateral movement before encryption phase
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Prometheus remote write with no authentication allowed attackers to delete metric samples and inject false "healthy" status

bug_bounty_reports.summary: InfluxDB timestamp backdating allows bypassing data retention policies and manipulating financial audit logs
Prometheus remote write authentication bypass enabling metric injection for monitoring evasion

security_research.title: Time Series Database Security: Protecting Against Data Manipulation Attacks
security_research.key_finding: 73% of exposed InfluxDB instances lack write authentication, enabling timestamp backdating and metric injection

security_research.title: Backdating Attacks in Financial Time Series Databases
security_research.key_finding: Timestamp manipulation is the #1 data integrity threat in financial TSDBs, affecting 41% of surveyed organizations

mitigations.title: Implement InfluxDB Token-Based Write Authentication with Timestamp Validation
mitigations.description: Enable InfluxDB 2.x authentication tokens with organization/bucket permissions and server-side timestamp validation to prevent backdating. Reject writes with timestamps outside acceptable drift window (±5 minutes) and enforce retention policies server-side.
nist_csf: PR.DS-6 - Integrity checking mechanisms are used
validation_steps: Generate InfluxDB API token with restricted bucket permissions
Test timestamp validation by attempting to write backdated metric (should fail)
Verify retention policy auto-deletes data after 30 days
Monitor audit logs for rejected write attempts with invalid timestamps
Test tag whitelist by attempting to write metric with unauthorized tag

mitigations.title: Enforce TimescaleDB Row-Level Security (RLS) with Timestamp Constraints
mitigations.description: Use PostgreSQL Row-Level Security policies in TimescaleDB to prevent timestamp manipulation and enforce write-once semantics for hypertables. Implement CHECK constraints to validate timestamps are within acceptable range.

code_example.code:
-- Create hypertable for IoT sensor data with timestamp constraints
CREATE TABLE sensor_readings (
  time TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  sensor_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  temperature DOUBLE PRECISION,
  humidity DOUBLE PRECISION,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  -- Prevent backdating: timestamp must be within 5 minutes of now
  CONSTRAINT valid_timestamp CHECK (
    time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '5 minutes' AND
    time <= NOW() + INTERVAL '5 minutes'
  ),
  -- Prevent future dating beyond reasonable NTP drift
  CONSTRAINT realistic_timestamp CHECK (time <= NOW() + INTERVAL '1 minute'),
  -- Validate sensor readings are realistic
  CONSTRAINT valid_temperature CHECK (temperature BETWEEN -50 AND 100),
  CONSTRAINT valid_humidity CHECK (humidity BETWEEN 0 AND 100)
);

-- Convert to hypertable with 7-day chunks
SELECT create_hypertable('sensor_readings', 'time', chunk_time_interval => INTERVAL '7 days');

-- Create retention policy (auto-delete after 30 days)
SELECT add_retention_policy('sensor_readings', INTERVAL '30 days');

-- Enable Row-Level Security
ALTER TABLE sensor_readings ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Create application role with restricted permissions
CREATE ROLE sensor_writer;
GRANT INSERT ON sensor_readings TO sensor_writer;
-- Deny UPDATE and DELETE to enforce write-once semantics
REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE ON sensor_readings FROM sensor_writer;

-- RLS Policy: Users can only insert data with current timestamp
CREATE POLICY sensor_insert_policy ON sensor_readings
  FOR INSERT
  TO sensor_writer
  WITH CHECK (
    -- Enforce timestamp is within 5 minutes of now (prevent backdating)
    time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '5 minutes' AND
    time <= NOW() + INTERVAL '5 minutes' AND
    -- Ensure created_at matches time (prevent timestamp forgery)
    ABS(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (created_at - time))) < 300
  );

-- RLS Policy: Prevent modification of historical data
CREATE POLICY sensor_no_update_policy ON sensor_readings
  FOR UPDATE
  TO sensor_writer
  USING (false); -- Deny all updates

CREATE POLICY sensor_no_delete_policy ON sensor_readings
  FOR DELETE
  TO sensor_writer
  USING (false); -- Deny all deletes

-- Create continuous aggregate for daily averages (protected from manipulation)
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW sensor_daily_avg
WITH (timescaledb.continuous) AS
SELECT
  time_bucket('1 day', time) AS day,
  sensor_id,
  AVG(temperature) AS avg_temp,
  AVG(humidity) AS avg_humidity,
  COUNT(*) AS reading_count
FROM sensor_readings
GROUP BY day, sensor_id
WITH NO DATA;

-- Refresh policy for continuous aggregate
SELECT add_continuous_aggregate_policy('sensor_daily_avg',
  start_offset => INTERVAL '3 days',
  end_offset => INTERVAL '1 hour',
  schedule_interval => INTERVAL '1 hour');

-- Create audit trigger to log all write attempts
CREATE TABLE sensor_audit_log (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  operation TEXT,
  sensor_id INTEGER,
  attempted_time TIMESTAMPTZ,
  actual_time TIMESTAMPTZ,
  user_name TEXT,
  client_ip INET,
  success BOOLEAN,
  error_message TEXT
);

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION audit_sensor_writes()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO sensor_audit_log (
    operation,
    sensor_id,
    attempted_time,
    actual_time,
    user_name,
    client_ip,
    success,
    error_message
  ) VALUES (
    TG_OP,
    NEW.sensor_id,
    NEW.time,
    NOW(),
    current_user,
    inet_client_addr(),
    true,
    NULL
  );
  RETURN NEW;
EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
  INSERT INTO sensor_audit_log (
    operation,
    sensor_id,
    attempted_time,
    actual_time,
    user_name,
    client_ip,
    success,
    error_message
  ) VALUES (
    TG_OP,
    NEW.sensor_id,
    NEW.time,
    NOW(),
    current_user,
    inet_client_addr(),
    false,
    SQLERRM
  );
  RAISE;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE TRIGGER sensor_audit_trigger
AFTER INSERT ON sensor_readings
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION audit_sensor_writes();

-- Example: Valid insert (current timestamp)
SET ROLE sensor_writer;
INSERT INTO sensor_readings (time, sensor_id, temperature, humidity)
VALUES (NOW(), 101, 22.5, 65.0);
-- SUCCESS

-- Example: Invalid insert (backdated timestamp - REJECTED)
-- INSERT INTO sensor_readings (time, sensor_id, temperature, humidity)
-- VALUES (NOW() - INTERVAL '60 days', 101, 22.5, 65.0);
-- ERROR: new row violates check constraint "valid_timestamp"

-- Example: Attempt to modify historical data (DENIED by RLS)
-- UPDATE sensor_readings SET temperature = 99.9 WHERE sensor_id = 101;
-- ERROR: new row violates row-level security policy for table "sensor_readings"

iso27001_controls: A.9.2.3 - Management of privileged access rights
nist_csf: PR.DS-6 - Integrity checking mechanisms are used
validation_steps: Create hypertable with timestamp CHECK constraints
Enable Row-Level Security and create restrictive policies
Test backdating attack (should fail with constraint violation)
Verify sensor_writer role cannot UPDATE or DELETE existing data
Review sensor_audit_log for failed write attempts
Confirm retention policy auto-deletes data after 30 days

mitigations.title: Secure Prometheus Remote Write with mTLS Authentication and Metric Validation
mitigations.description: Enable mutual TLS authentication for Prometheus remote write endpoints and implement metric validation rules to prevent injection attacks. Use recording rules to detect anomalous metric patterns (sudden spikes, timestamp gaps).

code_example.code:
# prometheus.yml - Secure remote write configuration with mTLS
global:
  scrape_interval: 15s
  evaluation_interval: 15s
  external_labels:
    cluster: 'production'
    region: 'us-east-1'

# Remote write to Thanos/Cortex with mTLS authentication
remote_write:
  - url: 'https://thanos-receive.example.com/api/v1/receive'
    # mTLS configuration
    tls_config:
      cert_file: /etc/prometheus/certs/client.crt
      key_file: /etc/prometheus/certs/client.key
      ca_file: /etc/prometheus/certs/ca.crt
      insecure_skip_verify: false
    # HTTP basic auth (additional layer)
    basic_auth:
      username: prometheus-writer
      password_file: /etc/prometheus/secrets/remote_write_password
    # Write relabeling for metric validation
    write_relabel_configs:
      # Drop metrics with suspicious patterns
      - source_labels: [__name__]
        regex: '.*[;\\|\$\(\)].*' # Prevent injection characters
        action: drop
      # Enforce required labels
      - source_labels: [job]
        regex: ''
        action: drop # Drop metrics without job label
      # Sanitize metric names
      - source_labels: [__name__]
        regex: '([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)'
        replacement: '${1}'
        target_label: __name__
    # Queue configuration to prevent DoS
    queue_config:
      capacity: 10000
      max_shards: 5
      min_shards: 1
      max_samples_per_send: 1000
      batch_send_deadline: 5s
      min_backoff: 30ms
      max_backoff: 100ms

# Recording rules for anomaly detection
rule_files:
  - /etc/prometheus/rules/*.yml

# Scrape config with authentication
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: 'kubernetes-nodes'
    scheme: https
    tls_config:
      ca_file: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/ca.crt
    bearer_token_file: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token
    kubernetes_sd_configs:
      - role: node
    # Relabel to enforce label standards
    relabel_configs:
      - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_node_name]
        target_label: instance
      - source_labels: [__address__]
        regex: '([^:]+)(?::\d+)?'
        replacement: '${1}:10250'
        target_label: __address__
---
# /etc/prometheus/rules/metric_validation.yml
groups:
  - name: metric_integrity_validation
    interval: 1m
    rules:
      # Detect timestamp anomalies (backdating)
      - alert: MetricTimestampAnomaly
        expr: |
          (
            timestamp(up) - time()
          ) > 300 or (
            time() - timestamp(up)
          ) > 300
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: critical
          category: data_integrity
        annotations:
          summary: "Metric timestamp drift exceeds 5 minutes"
          description: "Metric {{ $labels.job }}/{{ $labels.instance }} has timestamp drift of {{ $value }}s. Possible backdating attack."
      
      # Detect sudden metric value spikes (injection)
      - alert: AnomalousMetricSpike
        expr: |
          (
            rate(http_requests_total[5m]) / 
            rate(http_requests_total[5m] offset 1h)
          ) > 10
        for: 10m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          category: data_integrity
        annotations:
          summary: "Unusual 10x spike in metric values"
          description: "Metric {{ $labels.__name__ }} on {{ $labels.instance }} increased 10x. Possible metric injection."
      
      # Detect missing metrics (deletion attack)
      - alert: MetricGapDetected
        expr: |
          absent_over_time(up[10m])
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: critical
          category: data_integrity
        annotations:
          summary: "Critical monitoring metric disappeared"
          description: "Metric 'up' missing for {{ $labels.job }}/{{ $labels.instance }}. Possible deletion attack."
      
      # Detect tag manipulation
      - alert: UnexpectedLabelCardinality
        expr: |
          count(count by (__name__, job) (up)) by (__name__) > 100
        for: 15m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          category: data_integrity
        annotations:
          summary: "High label cardinality detected"
          description: "Metric {{ $labels.__name__ }} has >100 unique job labels. Possible tag injection attack."
---
# docker-compose.yml - Thanos Receive with authentication
version: '3.8'
services:
  thanos-receive:
    image: quay.io/thanos/thanos:v0.32.0
    command:
      - receive
      - --grpc-address=0.0.0.0:10901
      - --http-address=0.0.0.0:10902
      - --remote-write.address=0.0.0.0:19291
      - --tsdb.path=/var/thanos/receive
      - --label=receive="true"
      - --tsdb.retention=30d
      # mTLS configuration
      - --grpc-server-tls-cert=/certs/server.crt
      - --grpc-server-tls-key=/certs/server.key
      - --grpc-server-tls-client-ca=/certs/ca.crt
      # Enable authentication
      - --http.config=/etc/thanos/http_config.yml
    volumes:
      - thanos-receive-data:/var/thanos/receive
      - ./certs:/certs:ro
      - ./http_config.yml:/etc/thanos/http_config.yml:ro
    ports:
      - "10901:10901"
      - "10902:10902"
      - "19291:19291"
---
# /etc/thanos/http_config.yml - HTTP basic auth for remote write
basic_auth_users:
  prometheus-writer: $2y$10$X1234567890abcdefGHIJKL # bcrypt hash of password

tls_server_config:
  cert_file: /certs/server.crt
  key_file: /certs/server.key
  client_auth_type: RequireAndVerifyClientCert
  client_ca_file: /certs/ca.crt
---
# generate-certs.sh - Generate mTLS certificates
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Create CA
openssl genrsa -out ca.key 4096
openssl req -new -x509 -days 3650 -key ca.key -out ca.crt \
  -subj "/C=US/ST=CA/L=SF/O=Example/CN=Prometheus-CA"

# Create server certificate
openssl genrsa -out server.key 4096
openssl req -new -key server.key -out server.csr \
  -subj "/C=US/ST=CA/L=SF/O=Example/CN=thanos-receive.example.com"
openssl x509 -req -days 365 -in server.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key \
  -CAcreateserial -out server.crt

# Create client certificate
openssl genrsa -out client.key 4096
openssl req -new -key client.key -out client.csr \
  -subj "/C=US/ST=CA/L=SF/O=Example/CN=prometheus-client"
openssl x509 -req -days 365 -in client.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key \
  -CAcreateserial -out client.crt

echo "mTLS certificates generated successfully"
echo "Copy ca.crt, client.crt, client.key to Prometheus"
echo "Copy ca.crt, server.crt, server.key to Thanos Receive"

iso27001_controls: A.10.1.1 - Policy on the use of cryptographic controls
validation_steps: Generate mTLS certificates using OpenSSL script
Configure Prometheus remote_write with cert_file, key_file, ca_file
Deploy Thanos Receive with RequireAndVerifyClientCert
Test unauthenticated write attempt (should fail with TLS handshake error)
Create recording rules to detect timestamp anomalies
Verify MetricTimestampAnomaly alert fires when backdating is attempted
Monitor Thanos logs for rejected writes with invalid certificates

mitigations.title: Implement SIEM Detection for Time Series Data Manipulation
mitigations.description: Deploy SIEM queries to detect timestamp backdating, metric injection, and retention policy bypass across Azure Sentinel, Splunk, Elastic, and CloudWatch Insights.
validation_steps: Deploy KQL query in Azure Sentinel with 5-minute frequency
Create Splunk alert for FailedWrites > 20 in 1 hour
Configure Elastic EQL rule for RLS bypass detection
Set up CloudWatch Insights alarm for MaxDriftSeconds > 300
Test detection by simulating backdated write (should trigger alert within 5 minutes)

indicators: Sudden spike in database write operations from single IP address
Metrics with timestamps >30 minutes older than ingestion time
Retention policy jobs failing or being disabled
Continuous queries executing with unusual frequency (>1000/hour)
Tag cardinality explosion (10x increase in unique tag combinations)
Historical metrics changing values in immutable time ranges
Authentication failures on write endpoints followed by successful writes
Database user attempting UPDATE/DELETE on append-only time series tables

log_queries.query: InfluxDBLogs | where EventType == 'write' | extend TimestampDrift = abs(datetime_diff('second', EventTimestamp, ingestion_time())) | where TimestampDrift > 300 | summarize BackdatedWrites = count() by SourceIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m) | where BackdatedWrites > 10
event.category: 'database' AND event.action: 'INSERT' AND database.table: 'sensor_readings' AND script.result: 'timestamp_drift > 300'
index=timeseries_logs sourcetype=influxdb_write | eval drift=abs(_time - strptime(metric_timestamp, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")) | where drift > 1800 | stats count by src_ip, bucket | where count > 20
fields @timestamp, source_ip, timestamp_drift | filter event_type = 'metric_write' AND timestamp_drift > 300 | stats count(*) as backdated_writes by source_ip | filter backdated_writes > 10

tools.name: InfluxDB Audit Logs
tools.usage: Enable query logging in influxdb.conf: [logging] level = "debug", query-log-enabled = true

tools.name: TimescaleDB pg_audit Extension
tools.usage: CREATE EXTENSION pg_audit; ALTER SYSTEM SET pg_audit.log = 'write'; SELECT pg_reload_conf();

tools.name: Prometheus Alertmanager
tools.usage: Configure recording rules to detect timestamp anomalies and metric spikes
